citation_and_locator: Yip, Virginia and Stephen Matthews. 2000. Syntactic transfer in a Cantonese-English bilingual child. Bilingualism: Language and Cognition 3(3): 193-208; pp. 195-196, 201, 205-206
what_it_supports: Adult Cantonese permits discourse-recoverable null objects; exact transfer discussion contrasts this property with English and supplies contextual examples.
limit: The main study concerns bilingual acquisition; its null-topic analysis and child patterns do not authorize parser ontology or unrestricted adult omission.

citation_and_locator: Lee, Patrick Chi-Wai. 2018. A Study of Anaphoric Non-specific Object Drop in Cantonese: Classification and Derivation. Current Research in Chinese Linguistics 97(1): 245-257; pp. 247-255
what_it_supports: Direct definite object-drop dialogue and specific/non-specific contrast; existential, generic, and attributive non-specific subtypes; novel Cantonese examples checked with 20 Hong Kong-born speakers.
limit: NP-ellipsis with null-D is a theory-specific derivation; acceptability work does not establish every verb, context, or register.

citation_and_locator: Matthews, Stephen and Virginia Yip. 2011. Cantonese: A Comprehensive Grammar, 2nd ed.; CUHK companion website, chapter 5 section 5.1.2
what_it_supports: Direct omitted-pronoun example with an understood discourse-situation subject.
limit: Single teaching example does not establish a complete null-subject antecedent or clause-type matrix.

citation_and_locator: Holmberg, Anders. 2007. Null subjects and polarity focus. Studia Linguistica 61(3): 212-236; Cantonese examples 4b, 7-8 and section 6.3
what_it_supports: Cantonese is treated as a null-subject language, but a coreferential subject must be overt in the tested polarity-focus second conjunct; distinguishes short and long predicate replies.
limit: Specific polarity-focus theory and contexts do not supply a general null-subject licensing algorithm.

citation_and_locator: Lee, Tommy Tsz-Ming and Victor Junnan Pan. 2024. Licensing VP Movement and Ellipsis in Mandarin and Cantonese. Proceedings of WCCFL 40: 192-201; section 2 and Tables 1-2
what_it_supports: Cantonese VP ellipsis is selectively licensed: tested modal/auxiliary and Type-A aspectual heads contrast with progressive and Type-B aspectual heads.
limit: Theory-specific phase analysis and VP-level gaps do not determine null-NP arguments, fragment answers, or every lexical use.
